title: Key to Families of Indiana Coleoptera

lead_a.text: Hind femora greatly enlarged for jumping; small (<5 mm), often associated with leaf surfaces.

lead_b.text: Hind femora not enlarged for jumping, or insect larger than 5 mm.

lead_a.text: Antennae lamellate — terminal 3–7 segments expanded into one-sided plates that can spread or fold together.

lead_b.text: Antennae filiform, moniliform, serrate, clavate, or geniculate — not lamellate.

lead_a.text: Antennal club very large, the lamellae rigid and not closable; mandibles enormous and forcep-like in many males.

lead_b.text: Antennal club of 3–7 lamellae that can be folded together; mandibles of normal form.

lead_a.text: Prosternum produced posteriorly into a sharp peg that fits into a mesosternal pit; body capable of audible 'click'.

lead_b.text: Prosternum not produced as above; body unable to click.

lead_a.text: Tarsi 5-5-5; head prognathous with prominent mandibles; antennae filiform; pronotum with distinct lateral margins.

lead_b.text: Tarsal formula different, or head/antennae not as above.

lead_a.text: Body strongly elongate; antennae very long, often exceeding body length; tarsi appear 4-4-4 (cryptopentamerous).

lead_b.text: Body short-oval; head with elongate snout (rostrum); antennae geniculate and clubbed.
